 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.2","title":"Commission/generate hero illustration (assets/wills_estate_post_illustration.webp)","description":"Create the hero illustration for the article, paralleling `assets/slack_migration_post_illustration.webp`.

## Concept
A warm, editorial scene — a kitchen table under soft lamplight with a stack of important documents (will, deed, 401(k) form, a crypto hardware wallet, a passport), a laptop open showing an agent chat window, and a small glowing parrot perched on a chair back. The parrot is the visual anchor: it references Jeffreys X post line about "your beloved parrot that has another 20 years of life remaining." Warm purple/amber/emerald glow, same palette as the slack illustration.

## Workflow
1. Generate candidates via the preferred pipeline (likely Midjourney/SDXL/etc. via Jeffreys existing image-gen tooling).
2. Pick one, upscale, convert to WebP at ~560px wide display size (with 2x source for retina).
3. Place at `assets/wills_estate_post_illustration.webp`.
4. Reference from the article via `import illustration from "@/assets/wills_estate_post_illustration.webp"`.

## Acceptance criteria
- WebP file exists at the expected path.
- Used as the hero image via Next `<Image priority placeholder="blur">` pattern.
- Alt text is descriptive: "Warmly-lit desk scene with estate-planning documents, a laptop running an AI agent, and a parrot — evoking an estate plan drafted at home with the help of an AI skill."
- File size under ~700 KB (slack hero is 646 KB).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:36.015480119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:36.015480119Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.2","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:36.015480119Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.20","title":"Scaffold components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx","description":"Create `components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx` — "use client" module that exports all five named visualizations the article dynamic-imports.

## Scaffold scope
- `"use client"` directive.
- Imports: React (`useState`, `useEffect`, `useRef`, `useMemo`), framer-motion (`motion`, `AnimatePresence`, `useInView`, `useReducedMotion`), lucide-react icons, maybe `@react-three/fiber` or similar for 3D if any viz warrants it (probably not — 2D SVG/divs will suffice).
- Shared hooks: reduced-motion guard, mobile breakpoint detector (mirrors hooks already used in this repo).
- Five named exports (skeletons for now, implemented in downstream beads):
  - `TierTriageViz` — 5-rung wealth ladder
  - `AxiomCoherenceViz` — 8-spoke coherence diagram with mismatch toggle
  - `IntakePhasesViz` — 9-phase horizontal flow
  - `DeliverablesTreeViz` — 45-leaf deliverables tree
  - `AntiPatternCardsViz` — flip-card grid

## Why a single file
- Dynamic-imported with `{ ssr: false }` — wants to be one module for bundler locality.
- Mirrors slack articles pattern (`slack-migration-visualizations.tsx` is ~2500 lines with 4 visualizations).
- AGENTS.md discourages file sprawl. Keep everything article-specific in one viz module.

## Acceptance criteria
- File exists, all five named exports stubbed with placeholder `<div>` each so the article component can import them without runtime error.
- `bun tsc --noEmit` clean.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:40:53.483882643Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T00:47:15.453008335Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T00:47:15.450381004Z","close_reason":"Created components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx as a single client-only scaffold module with five named exports.

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.21","title":"TierTriageViz: interactive 5-tier wealth ladder","description":"Implement `TierTriageViz` in `components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx`.

## Visual design
- Vertical (mobile) / horizontal (desktop) ladder with 5 rungs.
- Each rung: title + net-worth band (e.g., "Tier 1 · Modest · < $500K"), archetype thumbnail, one-line goal summary.
- Default-selected tier: Tier 2 (middle-class) — because its the modal reader.
- Tapping a rung expands an inline detail panel with:
  - Archetype description (renter → dynasty)
  - Primary goals (guardianship → transfer-tax minimization)
  - Core deliverables (will + POAs → GRAT + IDGT + family constitution)
  - Typical complications (blended family, non-citizen spouse, business interests, vulnerable heirs)
- Active-rung accent color matches the design systems purple/cyan/emerald/amber/rose palette.

## Data
Pull the tier table verbatim from SKILL.mds "Wealth-Tier Triage" section:
- Tier 1 — Modest — < $500K — Young family, no business — Guardianship, basic will — Will, POA, healthcare directive, beneficiary audit
- Tier 2 — Middle-Class — $500K-$3M — Home + 401k + kids — Probate avoidance — + Revocable trust, TOD deed, pour-over will, 529 continuity
- Tier 3 — HNW — $3M-$15M — Home + investments + business + state-tax exposure — State estate tax, blended family, business succession — + QTIP/CST, ILIT, buy-sell, domicile planning
- Tier 4 — UHNW — $15M-$100M — Multi-asset, multi-state — Federal estate tax, GST, asset protection — + GRAT, IDGT, SLAT, dynasty trust, §6166, FLP
- Tier 5 — Industrialist — $100M+ — Operating empire, multi-generation, philanthropy — Multi-gen transfer, governance, cross-border — + Family office, private foundation, CLT/CRT, offshore, family constitution

## Complexity overlay
Below the ladder, a compact list of "what bumps you up" (blended family +1, non-citizen spouse +1, private business +1, vulnerable heir +1, multi-state/country +1, NFA firearms → specialty, self-custody crypto → specialty, etc.). Implement as a small chip row that highlights the current tier + overlay impact.

## Interactions
- Keyboard: arrow keys walk rungs, Enter to expand.
- Touch: tap to expand, tap-again to collapse.
- `prefers-reduced-motion`: no transitions, instant state change.

## Acceptance criteria
- Renders at all viewport sizes.
- Active-rung detail panel describes the tier accurately per SKILL.md.
- Fully keyboard-navigable and screen-reader-friendly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:14.352525529Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T01:03:41.629282344Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T01:03:41.626256376Z","close_reason":"Implemented TierTriageViz as an interactive five-rung wealth ladder with Tier 2 selected by default.

-{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.22","title":"AxiomCoherenceViz: 8-spoke diagram with mismatch mode","description":"Implement `AxiomCoherenceViz` in `components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx`.

## Visual design
- Radial/hub-and-spoke SVG diagram.
- Center node: "You" (your domicile + your plan).
- 8 spokes to asset / decision categories:
  1. Primary residence (house)
  2. 401(k) / employer retirement
  3. IRAs
  4. Life insurance
  5. Taxable brokerage
  6. Financial POAs
  7. Healthcare POAs + living will + HIPAA
  8. Digital inventory (email, password manager, crypto)
- Each spoke terminates at a small card showing "what document controls this" (will / trust / beneficiary form / titling / POA).

## The toggle
A pill at the top: **[Coherent] | [Incoherent]** — two preset states.

- **Coherent mode**: every spoke has a green check; the documents align.
- **Incoherent mode**: 3 spokes turn red with flashlight overlay:
  - 401(k) still names ex-spouse (Axiom 3 override).
  - House is joint-tenancy with an ex-sibling (Axiom 7, titling overrides will).
  - Crypto wallet has no seed-phrase pointer (Axiom 0 adjacent, but unrecoverable category).

## Interactions
- Click a spoke → inline paragraph beneath the viz explains what went wrong and which subagent catches it.
- Toggle: smoothly animates arrows + colors.
- Keyboard: tab through spokes, Enter to open detail.

## Why this viz
The whole core-insight section hinges on the reader *seeing* that coherence is the thing. A still-image diagram would be okay; an interactive toggle that literally shows the plan breaking is memorable.

## Acceptance criteria
- Mounts without SSR errors.
- Reduced-motion respected.
- Both modes are fully explorable (not just eye candy).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:28.844049716Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T01:15:01.001830658Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.22","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:28.844049716Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.22","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:15.345348997Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.23","title":"IntakePhasesViz: 9-phase flow with drill-in","description":"Implement `IntakePhasesViz` in `components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx`.

## Visual design
- Horizontal flow on desktop; vertical stack on mobile.
- 9 nodes: Orientation → People → Assets & Liabilities → Beneficiary Audit → Family Dynamics → Goals & Values → Incapacity → Jurisdiction → Wealth-Tier Routing.
- Each node shows:
  - Phase number + name
  - Approximate time (5 / 10 / 15 min per SKILL.md)
  - An icon from lucide-react
- Connected by arrows. Active phase highlighted.

## Scroll-linked highlighting
The articles sample-session section walks through a narrative that enters each phase in order. Use IntersectionObserver on paragraph markers to highlight the current phase as the reader scrolls.

## Click-to-drill
Click any node → inline panel under the viz shows:
- The agents opening question for that phase (plain English)
- The typical artifact written in that phase (e.g., `intake-record.md` updated, `red-flag-triage.md` created)
- Branch points (e.g., "if user says blended family, trigger overlay")

## Interactions
- Keyboard: arrow keys to walk, Enter to drill.
- Touch: tap to drill; the scroll-link still highlights the phase the reader is "in."
- Reduced motion: no arrow animations; still highlights correctly.

## Acceptance criteria
- Scroll-link works smoothly and doesnt flicker.
- Drill-in copy is accurate to SKILL.md section "The 9 Intake Phases."
- Responsive at 320px through 1440px.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:41.162156518Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T01:03:51.624956061Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.23","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:41.162156518Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.23","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:15.491176707Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":6,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.23","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW 2026-04-18: Scroll-linking implementation detail — use IntersectionObserver on [data-intake-phase='N'] markers placed by the sample-session section (hgjp.14).  {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.24","title":"DeliverablesTreeViz: 45-leaf interactive folder tree","description":"Implement `DeliverablesTreeViz` in `components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx`.

## Visual design
- Collapsible folder tree: top-level `my-estate-plan/` → three child folders `intake/`, `analyses/`, `deliverables/`.
- Leaf count: 2 + 20 + 23 = 45 files (exact match to the output contract in SKILL.md).
- Files represented as subtle pill rows with filename, icon, and tier-color (intake = gray, analyses = blue, deliverables = emerald).
- Mode-dependent files show a small "mode:" tag on hover (e.g., `business-continuity-activation.md` shows `mode: business-owner-succession`).

## Filters (toolbar above tree)
- **Show only**: All / Intake / Analyses / Deliverables.
- **Mode filter**: All / new-plan / existing-plan-audit / life-event-delta / urgent-bedside-signing / executor-activation / business-owner-succession / uhnw-restructure / maintenance-review.
- **Search input**: matches filename or plain-English purpose.

## Click-a-leaf behavior
Opens an inline detail card below the tree with:
- Filename
- 2-sentence plain-English purpose
- Representative snippet (2-5 lines) adapted from the template in `assets/` (lightly paraphrased for the web; we do not publish the full templates here — those are behind the paywall).
- Icon link to the relevant section of SKILL.md on the jeffreys-skills.md catalog page.

## Data source
A static const array at the top of the viz file, hand-curated from the bead Im reading now + `assets/` directory listing. Each entry: `{ path, category, purpose, modes[], sampleLines }`. Commit to writing all 45 entries by hand to keep them high-quality; do not scrape.

## Interactions
- Keyboard: tree is a proper `role="tree"` with up/down/left/right.
- Touch: tap to expand/collapse folders; tap leaves to open detail.
- Reduced motion: no expand/collapse animation, snap.

## Acceptance criteria
- All 45 leaves render.
- Filter combinations work (e.g., Mode=executor-activation shows fewer leaves highlighted).
- Detail card accurate.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:57.594122273Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:22:35.003617790Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.24","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:57.594122273Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.24","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:15.626927541Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.25","title":"AntiPatternCardsViz: 13-card flip grid","description":"Implement `AntiPatternCardsViz` in `components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx`.

## Visual design
- Responsive grid (4-col desktop / 2-col tablet / 1-col mobile) of flip-cards.
- ≥13 cards (one per anti-pattern listed in the anti-patterns section bead).
- Front of card: evocative pattern name + one-line hook + emoji/icon.
- Back of card: what actually goes wrong, worst-case outcome, which subagent the skill uses to catch it.

## Interactions
- Hover (desktop) or tap (mobile) to flip. Reduced motion: dissolve instead of 3D flip.
- Keyboard: Tab to focus, Space/Enter to flip.
- Cards are buttons with `aria-pressed` state for screen readers.
- On focus, a subtle ring appears matching the design system accent.

## Data source
Hand-authored array of `{ name, hook, explanation, worstCase, subagent }` objects. Use the 13-card starter list in the anti-patterns section bead.

## Style
Visual weight matches the slack articles callout cards. Each card has a subtle gradient border; hover glow.

## Acceptance criteria
- All cards flip.
- Reading the backs of the 13 cards gives the reader a comprehensive enough picture of the skills risk-scanning layer.
- Accessible: cards should be reachable and readable with keyboard alone.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:08.414151481Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:22:48.791039748Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.25","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:08.414151481Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.25","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:15.768018958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","title":"OpenGraph + Twitter images (app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/opengraph-image.tsx, twitter-image.tsx)","description":"Create the two image-route files for the article, modeled on the slack articles equivalents.

- URLs render correctly when shared on Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Slack, and iMessage.
- File sizes kept reasonable (<300 KB each).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:23.857413598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.052500801Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:23.857413598Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.051860361Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.34","title":"Content QA + proofreading pass before publish","description":"# Content QA + proofreading pass

Explicit human-review gate before the article goes live. The article is launching a paid product to a non-technical audience; typos and confusing copy directly translate to lost subscriptions.

## Review checklist
- [ ] Read the article end-to-end once on desktop, once on mobile.
- [ ] Every claim about the skill ("17 subagents", "45 deliverables", "8 operating modes", "9 intake phases", "11 axioms", "14 operators", "135 reference files", ~$15M 2026 exemption, OBBBA reference, etc.) is factually correct against the current SKILL.md.
- [ ] Every URL to jsm.md resolves — no 404s.
- [ ] Every `<J>` jargon tooltip renders a plain-English definition; no "?? undefined" in prod.
- [ ] Every section heading anchor (`#install`, `#who`, `#insight`, etc.) links from the TOC correctly.
- [ ] No typos, inconsistent capitalization, or duplicated sentences. Run the article copy through a proofing pass.

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.36","title":"Analytics: conversion events on article CTAs","description":"# Analytics events on article CTAs

Knowing which part of the article converts is how we iterate. Zero analytics = flying blind.

## Events to emit
Use whatever analytics the site already has (check `components/` for existing analytics wrappers; probably GA4 or Plausible). Emit:
- `article_viewed` (slug=wills-and-estate-planning, scroll_depth_bucket on unload: 0-25/25-50/50-75/75-100)
- `primer_download_clicked` (from the download CTA section)
- `install_section_cta_clicked` (target: "claude" | "codex" | "cli" | "manual-zip" — depending on which card the user clicks)
- `jsm_skill_page_outbound_clicked` (target: "wills-and-estate-planning-skill" or whichever URL was clicked)
- `toc_link_clicked` (anchor id)
- `visualization_interacted` (viz_name, action: "tier_selected" | "axiom_toggled" | "phase_drilled" | "deliverable_opened" | "antipattern_flipped")

All events include: referrer, session_id, article_slug, viewport_class ("mobile" | "tablet" | "desktop"). PII-free — no user_id (the article is public, so there IS no authenticated user on this page).

## Implementation
Define a tiny `emitEvent(name, props)` helper in `lib/analytics.ts` (or extend existing) if not already present. The article component calls it on the listed interactions.

## Privacy
Honour Do-Not-Track + the sites cookie-consent posture. If the site already has an analytics layer with opt-out wiring, inherit it automatically.

## Acceptance criteria
- Events observable in the analytics dashboard within 24h of deploy.
- No PII in any event.
- Dev-mode (NODE_ENV=development) can log events to console instead of sending, for debugging.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:00:04.316751104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:26:12.102826780Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.36","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:00:04.316751104Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.36","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:06.812792118Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.37","title":"FAQ section at end of article (anticipates common reader blockers)","description":"# FAQ section

Non-technical readers arrive with specific, predictable doubts. A FAQ section just before the TL;DR answers them inline rather than forcing them to email support or just abandon.

## Section structure (new id="faq" — add to TOC in bead .9)
SectionHeader eyebrow="FAQ" title="Questions you are about to ask."

## Question set (minimum — expand to ~15 during writing)
1. **Is this legal advice?** No — the skill produces audit + drafts + handoff packets. Your attorney signs.
2. **Do I still need an attorney?** Yes, for signing + state-specific formalities. The skill makes the attorneys job smaller, not optional. (Cross-reference section .18.)
3. **Is my data private?** The agent runs on your computer, not ours. The skill never transmits your financial documents anywhere — it reads local files and writes local files.
4. **What if Im not in the U.S.?** The skill is U.S.-only today. Foreign-asset overlays exist for U.S. domiciliaries with assets abroad, but if you are domiciled outside the U.S., this is not for you yet.
5. **Will the skill work if tax law changes?** The skill separates evergreen methodology from volatile law. Every live-law check is verified against primary sources and logged in `official-source-log.md`.
6. **Why $20/month instead of $20 once?** The skill is a living system — law updates, improvements, new subagents — that accrues value over time. Cancel anytime.
7. **What if I only need a simple will?** Youre probably better served by LegalZoom or Trust & Will. The skills value is in coordination across 8+ document types; if you just need one document, its overkill.
8. **How is this different from LegalZoom / Trust & Will / Rocket Lawyer?** (Link to the comparison section or paragraph — separate bead .38 if we build a full table.)
9. **Can I share the skill with my spouse / partner?** One subscription per user. Your spouse needs their own. The watermark on every bundle is tied to your user ID.
10. **What happens when I cancel?** You keep anything youve already produced — those are your files on your computer. You lose access to future updates + re-download. If you cancel mid-planning, you can finish offline with whats already on disk.
11. **Does it work on my phone?** Claude Code Desktop and Codex Desktop are Mac/Windows/Linux — no mobile app today. You need a computer.
12. **Can my attorney see what the skill produced?** Yes — the Attorney Engagement Brief is designed for exactly that. Take it to your lawyer; they will thank you.
13. **What if I get partway through and lose interest?** Your `intake-record.md` persists. Come back in a month, three months, or a year. The skill has a `maintenance-review` mode for periodic refresh.
14. **Is my crypto safe? Youre asking me about my seed phrase?** The skill NEVER asks for your seed phrase. The `digital-inventory.md` template has an explicit field that says "pointer to where your seed phrase is stored — do NOT put the seed phrase itself here."
15. **How long does this take?** A first full pass is typically 3-6 hours spread across a weekend, not a single sitting. Updates after life events take 30-60 min.

## Placement
Between the "Attorney handoff" section (.18) and the "TL;DR / pattern" section (.19). Add to the TOC anchor list.

## Accessibility
Each Q/A is a `<details>` element with `<summary>` for the question — native keyboard + screen-reader support, no JS needed.

## Acceptance criteria
- FAQ renders between .18 and .19.
- TOC includes the FAQ anchor.
- 15+ questions answered.
- Every Q has a plain-English A under 120 words.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:08:28.475969991Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T23:12:53.443996470Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.37","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:08:28.475969991Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.37","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:12:53.443371209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.38","title":"Comparison paragraph/table vs LegalZoom, Trust & Will, Rocket Lawyer","description":"# Comparison vs. competitor services

The $20/month price makes a serious reader ask "why not LegalZoom for $79?" Directly addressing this is the difference between the reader subscribing and bouncing.

## Placement
Inside the "Who this is for" section (.12) or just after it, as a compact comparison block. NOT a massive table — readers glaze at multi-column tables. Prefer a 4-5 row compact table or a scannable bulleted contrast.

## Content — three axes that matter
| Dimension | LegalZoom / Trust & Will / Rocket Lawyer | wills-and-estate-planning-skill |
|-----------|------------------------------------------|--------------------------------|
| Scope | Produces one document (a will, a trust, a POA) from a form-based intake | Coordinates will + trust + beneficiary forms + titling + POAs + directives + digital inventory so every document tells one coherent story |
| Coordination check | None — the will is produced in isolation from your 401(k), deeds, insurance | Beneficiary audit + titling audit + coherence audit catch the 80% of real-world failures before they happen |
| State-law verification | Static templates (may lag by months after law changes) | Verification-first discipline — live-law lookups against primary sources, logged |
| Attorney handoff | The document is the endpoint; if you want an attorney, start over with them | Every session ends with an Attorney Engagement Brief designed to cut your first billable meeting from 8-15 hours to 2-3 |
| Cost at similar depth | $79-499 per document, or ongoing subscription at $150+/yr for bundle | $20/month, cancel anytime |

## Honest positioning
Be explicit: "If you just need a single will and you have uncomplicated finances, LegalZoom or Trust & Will are fine. The skill is for the case where the will is not the whole plan — a blended family, a business, a vacation home in a second state, a vulnerable heir, a crypto wallet, or enough net worth that state estate tax matters."

Do NOT disparage the competitors. They serve a real need and the reader may already use them. Position jeffreys-skills.md as "when thats not enough."

## Acceptance criteria
- Comparison block exists either inside .12 or as a standalone callout between .12 and .13.
- Honest framing ("LegalZoom is fine for X; the skill is for Y").
- Readers understand the price anchor: a single LegalZoom will + estate plan typically runs $150-500; an attorney in person runs $3-30k; the skill is $20/month and pairs with whatever attorney you eventually bring in.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:08:50.908306116Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T23:12:53.645559420Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.38","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:08:50.908306116Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.38","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:12:53.644721059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.39","title":"Legal / UPL review pass on article + primer before publish","description":"# Legal / Unauthorized Practice of Law (UPL) review gate

The article markets a tool that produces estate-planning drafts. State bars take an active interest in unauthorized practice of law. Publishing language that can be construed as "this tool replaces a lawyer" is legally risky for Jeffrey personally AND for jsm.md as a business.

## Review checklist (applies to article + primer + install copy)
- [ ] No claim that the skill "writes your will" — only "produces drafts for attorney review."
- [ ] No claim that the skill "is legal advice" — explicit disclaimer in .18 + primer + FAQ.
- [ ] No attorney-client relationship implied or created.
- [ ] No state-specific guarantees ("Texas readers can sign this will" — WRONG; "Texas-specific execution formalities must be verified with Texas counsel" — RIGHT).
- [ ] No before/after comparisons that imply a specific outcome for a specific reader.

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.40","title":"Record + embed install-flow screencast in article install section","description":"# Embedded screencast/GIF of full install flow in the article

## Why
Non-technical readers will read "paste a prompt into Claude Desktop, the agent installs the skill" and picture something alien. A 30-60 second silent video showing the full flow on screen is an order of magnitude more convincing. Existing software marketing pages universally use this pattern.

## Content
Silent screencast (no audio needed; the article is read in a browser tab).

Sequence to capture:
1. On jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill, cursor hovers the "Generate install prompt" button; button pressed; textarea appears with the prompt visibly populated + countdown "Expires in 10:00".
2. "Copy" button pressed; subtle confirmation.
3. Switch window to Claude Desktop (or Codex Desktop — can produce two variants and A/B which feels clearer).
4. Paste into the chat input; Enter.
5. Agent's tool-use confirmation dialog appears; "Allow" clicked.
6. Agent's streaming response: "Downloading... Extracting... Verifying..."
7. On-screen success: agent says "The skill is installed. Reload skills to see it in the picker."
8. Cmd-Shift-P → "Reload skills" → skill appears.
9. User types "/wills" → skill invokes → opening prompt appears.

Approximate 45-second runtime. No voiceover. Subtitles/captions overlay at key moments ("Generate code", "Paste into Claude", "Agent installs", "Done — skill available").

## Production
- Record on mac-mini-max in 1440p, downscale for web.
- Edit with the existing video tooling (see the obs-youtube-music skill if Jeffrey uses it).
- Export formats:
  - MP4 (H.264): mainline, broad browser support.
  - WebM (VP9): smaller; Chromium-family browsers will prefer.
  - GIF fallback: for email preview + contexts that won't play video.
- Poster image: a single high-quality frame showing the install card mid-flow.

## Placement in the article
- Inline inside the install section (hgjp.11), immediately after the description of Card 1 "In Claude or Codex Desktop (recommended, no terminal)".
- Use Next's <video> component with:
  - poster={...}
  - preload="metadata" (saves bytes; user clicks play to load the full thing)
  - controls
  - muted + playsInline (for autoplay-on-scroll behaviour if desired, which I'd keep off by default — autoplay is annoying)
  - Captions via <track kind="captions">; author SRT or WebVTT alongside.
- Lazy-load: the <video> element only fully loads when scrolled into viewport.

## Accessibility
- Captions required (WCAG SC 1.2.2).
- An expandable text description of the same flow immediately below the video (serves screen readers + anyone who skips video).
- Reduced-motion: keep autoplay disabled; respect prefers-reduced-motion by showing the poster as a static image.

## Storage
- Asset file at assets/install-screencast.mp4 etc.
- Served directly from Next's public/ (or via a CDN reference if too large for public/).
- Target file sizes: MP4 < 5 MB, WebM < 3 MB. Higher-quality variants available as optional downloads for anyone who wants them.

## Tests
- Playwright: assert the video element is present, poster loads, captions track is wired.
- Manual QA: play on Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Mobile Safari. Play on reduced-motion. Play with autoplay disabled in browser settings.

## Acceptance criteria
- Screencast recorded, edited, captioned.
- Embedded in hgjp.11 install section.
- Static poster + expandable text description both present.
- No regressions on Lighthouse (bd-3t7zd.29 — uh, hgjp.29) — the video should not delay LCP since it's below-the-fold.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:06.571263626Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T00:23:56.988622402Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.40","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:06.571263626Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.40","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:02.880431851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.40","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:02.767148100Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.40","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW round-3 2026-04-19: Video production + hosting strategy.
(1) Hosting: do not host a multi-MB video on the same origin as the article — hurts LCP budget and Vercel bandwidth bills. Use Cloudflare Stream (generous free tier) or Mux (best DX). Absolute worst case, use Vercel Blob and accept the cost. The article's <video> src points at the CDN URL.
(2) Captions REQUIRED per WCAG SC 1.2.2. Author WebVTT (.vtt) alongside the video. Never ship without captions.
(3) Transcript below video: full text of the captions as a screen-reader-friendly expandable panel — also serves as fallback for users who skip video entirely.
(4) Poster image: a single well-composed frame (install card with prompt visible + countdown) — shows immediately; avoids the 'grey void while video loads' on slow connections.
(5) Do NOT autoplay. Respect user intent. Autoplay is annoying and violates the site's design ethos. The poster + play icon is enough affordance.
(6) Reduced-motion: if prefers-reduced-motion is set, show only the poster image + a link to 'Watch how the install flow works (45s video).' User must click through; no autoplay, no hover-preview.
(7) Dark/light mode: record under the same scheme as the rest of the article (dark background). Since it's a screen recording of jsm.md, the app's dark/light theme is what shows in the video. Pick the mode that looks best (likely dark) and stick with it.
(8) File sizes: MP4 (H.264) < 5 MB for compatibility; WebM (VP9) < 3 MB for bytesavers. Both served via <source>.","created_at":"2026-04-19T00:23:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","title":"Interactive pricing calculator (attorney vs LegalZoom vs jsm)","description":"# Interactive pricing calculator inside the article

## Why
The static comparison block (hgjp.38) puts the dollar amounts on the page, but readers don't internalise them unless they see their own numbers. An interactive calculator makes it visceral. 

The goal is not precision — the goal is "holy crap, that's a lot of money I could save."

## Placement
Inline inside the article, near or inside hgjp.38 (comparison section). NOT a separate section with its own anchor — this is a visual embedded widget, small and scannable.

## Inputs (minimal — two rows)
- Net worth slider: $100k → $100M (log scale); defaults to $1M.
- Family situation (multi-select chips, 0+ selected):
  - Blended family
  - Minor children or dependents
  - Business or partnership interest
  - Vacation home / multi-state property
  - Vulnerable heir (addiction, disability, capacity concerns)
  - Non-citizen spouse
  - Crypto self-custody
  - Concentrated stock / pre-IPO

## Outputs (three lines, typeset large)
- Attorney consultation estimate: derived from net worth + complexity factor
  - Formula (deliberately rough): base $3000 + $0.00005 per $ of net worth above $1M + $2000 per complexity chip.
  - Capped at $40k for the "HNW with every complication" reader.
- LegalZoom / Trust & Will estimate: static $199-499 regardless of inputs (they don't scale with complexity; that's precisely the point).
- Jeffreys Skills.md: $20/month (cancel anytime).

Below the three figures, a small footnote: "Attorney estimates are indicative only; actual quotes vary widely by state and firm. Our estimate uses public billing-rate data (cite one or two sources) for transparency."

## UX details
- Updates live as the user drags or toggles.
- Savings tag: "Projected savings vs attorney consult: $N,NNN." — animated on every input change.
- Smooth transitions respect prefers-reduced-motion.
- Keyboard-accessible: slider supports arrow keys, chips are toggle buttons.
- A11y: aria-live on the output row so screen readers get updates.

## Implementation
Pure client-side React component in components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx or a sibling file. No server round-trips; every calculation is local.

## Honest framing
Add a one-line disclaimer: "This calculator is a rough guide, not a legal fee quote. Actual attorneys quote based on actual facts. Your real skill subscription is $20/month — the attorney number is so you understand the order of magnitude."

## Tests
- Slider moves across its full range; every position produces a plausible number (no NaN, no negative).
- All 8 chips toggle independently; outputs reflect the change.
- Analytics: pricing_calc_changed (net_worth_bucket, num_chips) fires on each input; gives us insight into what readers actually have.

## Acceptance criteria
- Widget renders in the comparison area.
- All interactions smooth + accessible.
- Disclaimer explicit.
- Analytics events wired.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:32.001688634Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T00:23:57.084806847Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:32.001688634Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.38","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:03.312610349Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:03.206312451Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW round-3 2026-04-19: Pricing calc honesty + edge handling.
(1) Attorney rate data source citations: the formula's base + per-$ + per-chip coefficients must be derivable from public data (ABA billing-rate surveys, state bar reports). Add a sources paragraph below the calc: 'Rates derived from <ABA 2024 billing report>, <Martindale survey>, etc.' Without this the numbers feel pulled from thin air.
(2) Soft clamps: slider max is M; formula may produce silly numbers like k attorney for a k net worth (below the realistic -3k floor for a basic estate plan). Clamp outputs to a reasonable range (min k, max k for attorney; -499 for LegalZoom).
(3) Disclaimer proximity: the honest-framing sentence ('This calculator is a rough guide, not a legal fee quote') must be visually adjacent to the output numbers, not buried. Same font weight as the numbers themselves.
(4) No PII captured by the calc. Outputs derived client-side; analytics event records bucketed inputs (net_worth_bucket, num_chips) not raw values.
(5) Respect prefers-reduced-motion: slider live updates are animations; dampen them.
(6) Accessibility: slider is a native <input type='range'> with explicit min/max/step + aria-label; chips are toggle <button> with aria-pressed; outputs in aria-live='polite' region.","created_at":"2026-04-19T00:23:57Z"}]}
-{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.42","title":"Draft/preview mode for the article (pre-launch reviewer sharing)","description":"# Preview mode / share-token for pre-launch article

## Why
The legal / UPL review bead (hgjp.39), the content QA bead (hgjp.34), and the non-technical friend walkthrough (hgjp.34) all benefit from sharing a URL to the article BEFORE it ships. Also: Jeffrey might want one or two trusted reviewers (an attorney, a friend who read the tax article) to eyeball it before it hits Twitter.

Today, if we merge to main, Vercel publishes to production. There's no middle state.

## The design

### Option A: Vercel preview deployments (simplest)
Every PR or non-main branch auto-gets a preview URL from Vercel. Share that URL with reviewers. This already exists; the "new" thing is just using it deliberately.

Pro: zero code. Con: Vercel preview URLs are publicly accessible (no auth); anyone with the URL sees the article. For a launch article this is probably fine.

### Option B: Preview-token gate on the production site
Add a small middleware that checks ?preview=<token> query param OR a cookie. If the current article is within a "pre-publish" date window AND no valid token is present, serve a 404 (or a "launching soon" landing).

- Reserved tokens stored in a new small config in the repo (or in a database if jsm.md is doing this for all articles — but the personal-site is a static-first Next site, so a small table or env-backed list is fine).
- Tokens are opaque strings; minted on demand via a personal script.
- Token activates the article; without it the route is hidden.

### Recommended: Option A + small polish
Use Vercel preview deployments as the share mechanism. Add a lightweight `draft: true` frontmatter/flag to the article metadata; when draft, Next explicitly removes the article from /sitemap.xml, from the writing index (hgjp.5 registration), and from the command palette. When draft=false (publish day), the article surfaces everywhere.

This is the cleanest separation: draft state is a simple boolean in the article's metadata, reviewed in the content QA pass, flipped atomically at launch.

## Implementation

### Article metadata
```tsx
export const metadata = {
  title: "...",
  description: "...",
  alternates: { canonical: "/writing/wills-and-estate-planning" },
  robots: { index: false, follow: false }, // while draft
};

const DRAFT = true; // flip to false on launch day
```

### lib/content.ts
Skip draft articles when building the writing index:
```ts
if (!post.draft) writingList.push(post);
```

### sitemap.ts
Same: omit draft articles.

### command-palette.tsx
Same: omit draft articles from search index.

### Publishing
- Throughout development, draft=true; shareable via vercel-<branch>-<sha>.vercel.app URLs.
- Launch commit flips draft=false + robots: index, follow.

## Tests
- Playwright: in draft mode, /writing does not list the article; /sitemap.xml does not include it; Cmd-K "wills" returns no result. Yet direct visit to /writing/wills-and-estate-planning still renders (for authorized reviewers sharing the URL).
- Launch mode (draft=false): article appears in /writing, /sitemap.xml, command palette.

## Acceptance criteria
- draft flag implemented and wired to all three surfaces.
- Publishing is one commit: flip the flag.
- Search engines do not index draft articles (meta robots noindex).
- Tests cover both states.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:59.171182267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:22:54.774983884Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.42","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:59.171182267Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.42","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW round-3 2026-04-19: Draft-mode safety belt.
(1) meta robots: while draft=true, the page emits <meta name='robots' content='noindex, nofollow'>. Verified via Playwright test that fetches the draft URL and asserts the header.
(2) Accidental index monitoring: add a simple once-daily cron that fetches the draft article URL, inspects the response for the noindex meta tag; if missing, page the operator. Catches the case where someone flips draft=false earlier than intended.
(3) The draft flag must be checked in THREE places per the bead description: sitemap.ts, lib/content.ts writing list, command-palette search index. Test all three — easy to miss one.
(4) Preview URL shareability: Vercel preview URLs are guessable enough that a reviewer who shares a screenshot can't inadvertently leak the URL via metadata. If that's a concern, add HTTP Basic auth to Vercel preview (Vercel Pro feature) or shrink the review window.
(5) Launch procedure: the commit that flips draft=false should ONLY flip that flag. Not bundled with other changes. Diff is one line; trivially reviewable.
(6) Post-launch: after the draft flag flips and the article is live, remove the robots-noindex cron check (it's no longer needed).","created_at":"2026-04-19T00:23:57Z"}]}

(3) The draft flag must be checked in THREE places per the bead description: sitemap.ts, lib/content.ts writing list, command-palette search index. Test all three — easy to miss one.
(4) Preview URL shareability: Vercel preview URLs are guessable enough that a reviewer who shares a screenshot can't inadvertently leak the URL via metadata. If that's a concern, add HTTP Basic auth to Vercel preview (Vercel Pro feature) or shrink the review window.
(5) Launch procedure: the commit that flips draft=false should ONLY flip that flag. Not bundled with other changes. Diff is one line; trivially reviewable.

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","title":"Write agent-readable primer markdown (public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md)","description":"Write `public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md` — a single-file primer that a paying subscriber can download from the article and paste into any fresh Claude / Codex / ChatGPT session to boot the agent into the wills-estate-planning mindset.

## Purpose
This is the offline-friendly equivalent of the skill itself. The full skill is 201 files, ~24k lines, ~1 MB — too big to paste into a context window. The primer is a lossy but coherent summary the agent can use even without the full skill installed. Acts as:

1. **Proof-of-value tease** for visitors who are not yet paying subscribers (they can see the shape of the skill).
2. **Emergency fallback** for a paying subscriber whose agent setup isnt cooperating — they can paste this and still get useful output.
3. **The thing the "Download the operator primer as Markdown" button on the article points at** (see `PRIMER_HREF` in the article scaffold bead).

## Structure (draws from SKILL.md at ~/jeffreys_skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill/)
- `# Wills & Estate Planning — Operator Primer`
- TL;DR: what estate planning really is (coordination, not drafting), the 11 axioms condensed to the 5 most important ones, and the 2026 OBBBA $15M exemption reality.
- The kernel: axioms 0-11 in one-liner form with a sentence of why each matters.
- Wealth-tier routing (Tier 1-5 with one sentence each).
- The 9 intake phases as a numbered list.
- The 9-step workflow as a numbered list.
- The top 15 anti-patterns.
- The verification-first discipline (live-law checks).
- Output contract summary (intake/, analyses/, deliverables/).
- A short disclaimer: this primer is educational; an attorney signs the documents.

## Format
- Plain Markdown.
- ~8-12 KB (smaller than slack primers 185 KB — this is deliberately a condensed version, not a complete operator guide).
- Semantic headings so an agent can index it.

## Acceptance criteria
- File exists at `public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md`.
- Articles download button successfully serves it as `application/octet-stream` with the configured filename.

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hpr","title":"Two Worlds Narrative Section","description":"# Epic: "Two Worlds" Narrative Section

## Overview
Create a section that bridges Jeffrey's finance background with his AI tooling work, showing how the former informs the latter.

## Strategic Rationale
- Jeffrey has a rare combination: mathematician + hedge fund analyst + AI infrastructure builder
- Most visitors will see these as separate worlds
- The truth: Wall Street pattern recognition directly informs how he builds AI tools
- This positioning differentiates him from pure engineers or pure finance people

## The Mapping
| Wall Street Pattern | → | Agentic AI Application |
|---------------------|---|------------------------|
| Information edge through exhaustive research | → | CASS: Never re-solve a problem |
| Risk management across correlated positions | → | SLB: Peer review for dangerous ops |
| Portfolio coordination across PMs | → | Agent Mail: Multi-agent coordination |
| Factor models reveal hidden exposure | → | UBS: Static analysis reveals hidden bugs |
| Position sizing based on conviction | → | Beads: Prioritize by graph metrics |

## Narrative Thread
"A decade of pattern recognition at hedge funds trained a particular way of seeing systems. When I started building tools for AI coding agents, that same lens revealed structural problems that pure software engineers might miss."

## Placement Options
1. **About page**: New section after career timeline
2. **Homepage**: Below the fold, above projects
3. **Dedicated page**: /philosophy or /approach

## Recommended: About Page
- Natural flow from career timeline to "how it informs my current work"
- Doesn't clutter homepage
- Deep enough content to warrant dedicated reading

## Design Approach
- Visual: Split-screen or alternating cards
- Left side: Finance concept (darker, corporate feel)
- Right side: AI tool application (tech feel, gradients)
- Arrow or transition between them

## Success Criteria
- Visitors understand the connection within 10 seconds
- Feels insightful, not forced
- Enhances credibility in both domains","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:05:34.786800125Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T08:05:51.708053218Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T08:05:51.708053218Z","close_reason":"Implemented in commit b1cb57c: Two Worlds section with parallax effects, skill transfer cards; GitHub Heartbeat widget with live activity feed, animated heartbeat visualization, streak badges","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}
